prompt:
The notification templates read like a robot wrote them: '1 files uploaded', '3 persons mentioned'. I want an inflection module, inflect.ts, built around rule tables like Rails' Inflector — not a wall of if-statements.

Export class Inflector plus a ready default instance and bound helpers: export const inflector, and export function pluralize / singularize that delegate to it. Every new Inflector() starts with the built-in tables.

Inflector API:
- pluralize(word, count?) — with no count, always the plural. With a count, exactly 1 yields the singular form, anything else the plural
- singularize(word)
- addIrregular(singular, plural), addUncountable(word), addPluralRule(regexp, replacement), addSingularRule(regexp, replacement) — replacements use $1 capture syntax; rules added later WIN over built-ins (the tests add /(ind)ex$/ -> '$1ices' and expect 'indices' even though the default table would say 'indexes')

Lookup order both directions: uncountables first, then irregulars, then the rule table, newest rule first.

The built-in table has to cover what the tests exercise (test_inflect.ts, run `node --test test_inflect.ts`): plain +s; -s/-x/-z/-ch/-sh -> -es (bus/buses, box/boxes, church/churches, status/statuses); consonant+y -> -ies but vowel+y just adds s (city/cities, day/days, key/keys); knife/knives, wife/wives, life/lives, leaf/leaves, wolf/wolves; analysis/analyses, crisis/crises; irregulars person/people, child/children, mouse/mice, foot/feet, tooth/teeth; uncountables sheep, fish, series, information, equipment. All of those singularize back too.

Also: pluralizing an already-plural irregular is a no-op ('people' stays 'people'), casing is preserved for Capitalized and ALLCAPS words, and custom irregulars/uncountables work in both directions.

Tests are the contract; leave them untouched. Zero deps.
